cards.title: Core Concepts of Life Insurance
cards.content:
• Human Life Value (HLV) - Prof. Hubner
• Mutuality and Risk Pooling
• Level Premium System vs. Natural Premium

cards.title: 1. What is HLV?
cards.content: Developed by Prof. Hubner. It measures a person's economic value based on future net earnings. It answers: 'How much insurance do I need?'

cards.title: 2. Risk Pooling & Mutuality
cards.content: Foundation of insurance. The 'Many' contribute to a common fund to share the losses of the unfortunate 'Few'.

cards.title: 3. Natural vs. Level Premium
cards.content:
Natural Premium: Increases every year as risk (age) increases.
Level Premium: Remains constant throughout the term. Policyholders pay MORE in early years to cover high risk in later years.

cards.title: 4. Cash Value & Reserve
cards.content: In Level Premium, the excess money collected in early years creates a reserve. This build-up is known as the 'Cash Value' or 'Surrender Value'.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: System with constant premium: Level Premium System.
Cash Value Reason: Excess premium collected in early years of Level Premium policies.
Life Insurance Contract: Long-term protection + savings.
